# Orchestration

**Slug:** `orchestration` · **Version:** `2021-03` · **Tools:** 90

Manage your Artifacts, Tasks, Plans, Schedules, Environments, Workspaces, Promotions and all associated Ressources

### `orchestration__getAvailableArtifacts`

```
GET /orchestration/artifacts
```

Get available Artifacts

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `environmentId` | query | `string` | no | environment id |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `workspaceId` | query | `string` | no | workspace id |
| `name` | query | `string` | no | artifact name filter |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `types` | query | `"standard" | "big_data_streaming" | "big_data_batch" | "route" | "data_service" | "pipeline" | "data_fabric"[]` | no | artifact types, can use multiple values |

## environments

### `orchestration__createEnvironment`

```
POST /orchestration/environments
```

Create Environment

Create an Environment. 

Requirements: 
* environment name must be unique
* the user account issuing the request must be granted the ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT permission

Environment is created on platform. 
A CUSTOM workspace is automatically created within this environment with proper ownership permissions assigned onto it. 

Upon your request, Cloud Engine could also be assigned to the newly added environment.  


_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__deleteEnvironment`

```
DELETE /orchestration/environments/{Id}
```

Remove Environment

Remove the identified Environment and all its subparts : workspaces, executables (tasks and plans), etc. 

Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

Environment *in use* won't be removed : if any engine is assigned to this environment or if any promotion relates to this environment, removal is not achieved. 

**Please make sure Environment removal is confirmed before calling this endpoint as removal will be cascading all subsidiary elements within the environment : workspaces, tasks, plans, etc. With no cancellation service available. **


**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `Id` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__getAvailableEnvironments`

```
GET /orchestration/environments
```

List Environments

Retrieve all the environments according to filters.

If the user is entitled with CLUSTER_MANAGEMENT or ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role, all environments matching query will be returned.

If the user is entitled with OPERATOR role, environments matching query and the user workspace permissions will be returned.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `query` | query | `string` | no | search query (FIQL format), e.g. name==Dev*;description==*environment* |


### `orchestration__updateEnvironment`

```
PUT /orchestration/environments/{Id}
```

Update Environment

Update the identified Environment.
Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `Id` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.

### `orchestration__getPlanRunConfiguration`

```
GET /orchestration/executables/plans/{planId}/run-config
```

Get Plan configuration

Get Plan configuration applied to your Plan executions. 

Please take note : 
* the Trigger definition can now have some new value 'Multiple' defined when you link your plan to some Multiple Time Trigger schedule. 
* whenever such multiple trigger schedule is linked please use the new dedicated endpoints to manage this specific scheduling configuration.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planId` | path | `string` | yes | plan id |

## schedules

### `orchestration__appendTrigger`

```
POST /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}/triggers
```

Append some trigger to your schedule

Append some elementary trigger in the list. Some pre-conditions are checked : 
* the Trigger object must be valid
* Trigger name provided must be unique 
* you can add up to 15 elementary triggers in one schedule
* the Trigger can't be identical to one existing trigger : no duplicate allowed
* the new Trigger added should not bring new scheduled events that are concurrent to current events in the existing schedule : interval between two executions of the same task must not be less than 5 minutes. The concurrency of triggers are checked up to 5 years, allowing bissextile years case to be taken into consideration.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `ScheduleTrigger`. Schema: `ScheduleTrigger`.


### `orchestration__cloneSchedule`

```
POST /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}
```

Clone the schedule

Duplicate the current schedule : all the triggers present in the source schedule will be duplicated on the copy. 
If no name is provided for the schedule copy, some "copy of" source name will be given to target schedule copy. 
The newly created schedule wont be linked to any executable, but ready to be. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__createSchedule`

```
POST /orchestration/schedules
```

Create a schedule

Create a new schedule providing one initial trigger item. 

You would be able to add some more triggers, update one trigger or remove it using the unique ressource endpoint for your created schedule. 

The created schedule is orphan, it means it is not related yet to any task or plan for your executions. 

User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission at least for one workspace in the environment.

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__deleteSchedule`

```
DELETE /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}
```

Delete the schedule

Delete the schedule object. Please note only orphan Schedules can be deleted. When the schedule is being used by an executable, It cannot be deleted. 
Therefore,  when you need to perform a schedule cleanup, unlink your schedule before removing it. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__deleteTrigger`

```
DELETE /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}/triggers
```

Remove a trigger from your schedule

Remove some trigger from the list within this schedule.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |
| `name` | query | `string` | yes | Name of the trigger to remove |


### `orchestration__getAvailableSchedules`

```
GET /orchestration/schedules
```

Get all schedules

Retrieve schedules present on your account according to several optional search criteria :
* Environment
* Description
* Bound status : filter in or out orphan schedules or schedules in action on a task or plan

Please be aware user would need VIEW permission on the workspace where the schedule is in action and at least on one workspace within the environment for unlinked schedules.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `descriptionFilter` | query | `string` | no | Search in Description with regular expression |
| `environmentId` | query | `string` | no | Environment to narrow the search |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `onlyBound` | query | `boolean` | no | Only bound schedulers, ie in use for scheduling some executable |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |


### `orchestration__getPlanSchedule`

```
GET /orchestration/executables/plans/{planId}/schedule
```

Retrieve plan schedule

Retrieve any Schedule definition associated to the Plan. 

User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have VIEW Permission on the workspace this plan belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__getSchedule`

```
GET /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}
```

Retrieve the schedule

Retrieve whole details of the schedule definition.

Please be aware user would need VIEW permission on the workspace when the schedule is in action on some task or plan.
When not in action, User should have VIEW permission at least on one workspace within the environment.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__getTaskSchedule`

```
GET /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/schedule
```

Retrieve task schedule

Retrieve the schedule associated to this task. User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have VIEW Permission on the workspace this task belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__linkPlanSchedule`

```
PUT /orchestration/executables/plans/{planId}/schedule
```

Associate a schedule to a Plan.

Apply a schedule to the run configuration of this Plan. When some trigger is already in run-config for this plan, the new schedule object will be replacing it. 
User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission on the workspace this plan belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__linkTaskSchedule`

```
PUT /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/schedule
```

Associate a schedule to a Task.

Apply a schedule to the executions of a Task. 
When some trigger is already in run-config for this task, the new schedule object will be replacing it right away. 

User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission on the workspace this task belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__simulateNextTriggeredEvents`

```
GET /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}/events
```

Simulate next triggered events

Retrieve all the events that would be triggered by the schedule for a given date-time period. When no first datetime provided, the current datetime is taken as default value.
This endpoint is useful for checking the design of your schedule before putting it in use or when some changes is needed.  

Each triggered event is provided with the name of the elementary trigger that generated it : using this information you would be able to easily adapt the right trigger when needed. 

User should have VIEW permission on the workspace when the schedule is in action on some task or plan.
When not in action, User should have VIEW permission at least on one workspace within the environment. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | yes | Date time (milliseconds) limit to compute next events till |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | Date time (milliseconds) to compute next events from. |


### `orchestration__unlinkPlanSchedule`

```
DELETE /orchestration/executables/plans/{planId}/schedule
```

Remove the association between the Schedule and the Plan

Unassign a schedule to a Plan. Once unassigned no further scheduled execution will happen. Your plan would still be executed through direct calls (UI or API).
User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission on the workspace this plan belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__unlinkTaskSchedule`

```
DELETE /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/schedule
```

Remove the association between a Task and a Schedule

Unassign a schedule to a Task. Once unassigned no further scheduled execution will happen. Your task would still be executed through direct calls (UI or API) or within some plan. 

User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission on the workspace this task belongs to.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__updateSchedule`

```
PATCH /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}
```

Update the schedule

Apply partial changes to the scheduler object. Actually allows you to update the functional description of your schedule. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.


### `orchestration__updateTrigger`

```
PUT /orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}/triggers
```

Update an existing trigger within the schedule

Apply changes to an existing trigger in the list. The same preconditions are applied as when adding some new trigger. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `scheduleId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `ScheduleTrigger`. Schema: `ScheduleTrigger`.


## tasks

### `orchestration__changeTaskStatus`

```
PUT /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/pause
```

Pause / Resume a Task

Allow to pause or resume a Task. 
When pausing a task all current executions will be completed, all new executions requests will be discarded, including scheduled, manual (through TMC or API) and webhook ones. 
When resuming a task all triggers (cron, webhook, etc.) will be reactivated, all new executions requests will be accepted again.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `TaskPauseDetails`. Schema: `TaskPauseDetails`.


### `orchestration__configureDETaskExecution`

```
PUT /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/de-run-config
```

Configure Task executions for dynamic engine

Configure how tasks are executed on Dynamic Engines.
* Permission required:  `TMC_OPERATOR`.
* Timeout option: Set a default timeout value (in minutes) to terminate task executions that exceed this duration. Note: Accuracy is within 10 seconds.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes | task id |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `DETaskRunConfig`. Schema: `DETaskRunConfig`.


### `orchestration__configureTaskExecution`

```
PUT /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/run-config
```

Configure Task executions

Configure Task regarding executions.
<br>User must be granted `TMC_OPERATOR` permission
<br>Timeout default value can be set as option : it would apply for terminating all executions of this task whose duration exceeds the time out provided in minutes. Accuracy is about 10s. Please note that this capability is available for CE, REC and RE version 2.12.2 and onwards. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes | task id |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `TaskRunConfig`. Schema: `TaskRunConfig`.


### `orchestration__createTask`

```
POST /orchestration/executables/tasks
```

Create Task

Create Task. By default the created task will have 'CLOUD' as its execution environment

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `TaskV21CreateRequest`. Schema: `TaskV21CreateRequest`.


### `orchestration__deleteTask`

```
DELETE /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}
```

Delete Task

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes | task id |


### `orchestration__getAvailableTasks`

```
GET /orchestration/executables/tasks
```

Get available Tasks

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `environmentId` | query | `string` | no | environment id |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `runtimeRunProfileId` | query | `string` | no | runtime run profile id |
| `runtimeType` | query | `"CLOUD" | "REMOTE_ENGINE" | "REMOTE_ENGINE_CLUSTER" | "CLOUD_EXCLUSIVE" | "PIPELINE_ENGINE"` | no | runtime type |
| `workspaceId` | query | `string` | no | workspace id |
| `name` | query | `string` | no | task name filter |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `runtimeId` | query | `string` | no | runtime id |
| `artifactId` | query | `string` | no | artifact id |

```
GET /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/de-run-config
```

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### `orchestration__getTaskConfiguration`

```
GET /orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId}/run-config
```

Get Task configuration

Get Task configuration currently applied. 
Please take note : 
* the Trigger definition can now have some new value 'Multiple' defined when you link your task to some Multiple Time Trigger schedule. 
* whenever such multiple trigger schedule is linked please use the new dedicated endpoints to manage this specific scheduling configuration.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes | task id |


Get task scheduled triggers

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | yes | to date time (milliseconds) |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | from date time (milliseconds) |

## tasks 

### `orchestration__listAvailableTasks`

```
POST /orchestration/executables/tasks/search
```

Search tasks

Search Tasks according to some filters:
* name
* environment id
* workspace id
* artifact id 
* runtime type
* runtime id
* runtime runprofile id
* tags
* trigger type

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience, apart from Environment and Workspace which are exclusive (ie you should not provide both in a same call)

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `TaskFiltersRequest`. Schema: `TaskFiltersRequest`.


## untagged

### `orchestration__post_orchestration_executables_promotions_promoti`

```
POST /orchestration/executables/promotions/{promotionId}
```

Perform promotion analysis

Simulate upcoming executions of a promotion and get reports about its change analysis without executing it in real. Note that the target version displayed in the analysis could differ from the target version from the real promotion execution, if some changes are made in the target environment between the simulation and the actual execution of the promotion.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `promotionId` | path | `string` | yes | Promotion ID |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `PromotionExecutionDetails`. Schema: `PromotionExecutionDetails`.


## workspaces

### `orchestration__createCustomWorkspace`

```
POST /orchestration/workspaces
```

Create Workspace

Create a Workspace. 

Please ensure the following requirements are met :
* provide environment this workspace will belong to
* provide some unique naming within environment 
* user must be granted ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT permission

Owner can be provided. By default, creator user would be owner. 
Type is Custom. 

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `CreateWorkspaceRequest`. Schema: `CreateWorkspaceRequest`.


### `orchestration__deleteWorkspace`

```
DELETE /orchestration/workspaces/{id}
```

Remove Workspace

Remove the Workspace and all its subparts : executables (tasks and plans), permissions, etc. 

Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role. 

Custom Workspaces only can be removed using this API. (Shared and Personal are linked either to Environment or User's life cycle)

Workspace in use won’t be removed : if any task, artifact, resource, connection is used in any other workspace, removal is not achieved. Please make sure Workspace removal is confirmed before calling this endpoint as removal will be cascading all subsidiary elements within. With no cancellation service available.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `orchestration__getAvailableWorkspaces`

```
GET /orchestration/workspaces
```

List Workspaces

Retrieve all the workspaces according to filters.

If the user is entitled with CLUSTER_MANAGEMENT or ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role, all workspaces matching query will be returned.

If the user is entitled with OPERATOR role, workspaces matching query and the workspace permissions will be returned.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `query` | query | `string` | no | This parameter defines a search query in FIQL format. The Feed Item Query Language is a syntax for expressing filters across the entries. |


### `orchestration__updateCustomWorkspace`

```
PUT /orchestration/workspaces/{id}
```

Update Workspace

Update the identified Workspace.
Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `object`.
